reflectYourStyle.question: When a crisis hits the team, your first instinct is to:

options.A: Describe the big-picture goal to keep people inspired.
options.B: Sit down with individuals to see how they are coping.
options.C: Call a meeting to gather everyone's input on the solution.
options.D: Jump in and show them exactly how to fix it by doing it yourself.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you prefer to give feedback?

options.A: By reminding them how their work fits into the long-term mission.
options.B: By asking them how they think they can improve their own skills.
options.C: By discussing it openly as a group to reach a consensus.
options.D: By pointing out high-performance gaps and demanding immediate results.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your team is stuck on a complex strategic decision. You:

options.A: Provide a clear 'North Star' and let them find the path.
options.B: Use it as a 'teachable moment' to build their problem-solving skills.
options.C: Facilitate a brainstorm where every voice has equal weight.
options.D: Set a tight deadline and provide the specific data they must use.

reflectYourStyle.question: What does 'Success' look like to you at the end of a project?

options.A: We realized a bold new vision that didn't exist before.
options.B: Every team member grew personally and professionally.
options.C: The team feels a deep sense of ownership and agreement.
options.D: We hit every KPI ahead of schedule with zero errors.

reflectYourStyle.question: A high-potential employee is underperforming. Your approach is:

options.A: Re-align them with the 'Why' behind their role.
options.B: Spend 1-on-1 time helping them unlock their own roadblocks.
options.C: Ask the team how they can support this person.
options.D: Give them a 'stretch goal' and hold them strictly accountable.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a meeting, you find yourself doing mostly:

options.A: Painting a picture of the future.
options.B: Asking open-ended questions.
options.C: Listening and summarizing the group's thoughts.
options.D: Giving directions and setting the tempo.

reflectYourStyle.question: When planning for next year, you focus most on:

options.B: Succession planning and talent development.
options.C: Building a culture of collaboration and trust.
options.D: Operational excellence and efficiency gains.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your team would describe your leadership 'Presence' as:

options.B: Supportive and Empathic.
options.C: Inclusive and Fair.
options.D: Intense and Result-Oriented.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you handle 'Systems Thinking' in your organization?

options.A: I look for how the brand image impacts the market system.
options.B: I look at how the human relationships affect the workflow.
options.C: I look at how democratic participation improves the whole system.
options.D: I look for bottlenecks in the execution chain.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your ultimate goal as a leader is to:

options.B: Be a developer of people.
options.C: Be a builder of communities.
options.D: Be a driver of performance.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a team member disagrees with you, you:

options.A: Explain the long-term benefits of your vision.
options.B: Ask them to explain their perspective to help them learn.
options.C: Seek a middle ground that everyone can live with.
options.D: State that the decision is final to maintain momentum.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you define accountability?

options.A: Living up to the organization's core values.
options.B: Taking responsibility for one's own professional growth.
options.C: Being answerable to the rest of the team.
options.D: Meeting specific, pre-defined metrics and targets.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the best way to motivate a discouraged team?

options.A: Tell a compelling story about the impact of their work.
options.B: Have 1-on-1 heart-to-hearts to build confidence.
options.C: Hold a group session to let everyone vent and regroup.
options.D: Set a small, easy-to-win goal to get a 'quick victory'.

reflectYourStyle.question: During a strategic pivot, you focus on:

options.A: Finding the 'New North' for the organization.
options.B: Ensuring no one feels left behind by the change.
options.C: Getting a vote on the new direction.
options.D: The speed of the transition to avoid lag.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your approach to delegation is:

options.A: Give them the goal, not the instructions.
options.B: Give them tasks that will challenge their current skills.
options.C: Give the task to whoever the group thinks is best.
options.D: Give precise instructions and a strict deadline.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you enter a room, you want people to feel:

options.B: Heard and understood.
options.C: Like they are part of a team.
options.D: Ready to get to work immediately.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a performance review, you spend the most time on:

options.B: Asking what they want to learn next.
options.C: Discussing team dynamics and relationships.
options.D: Reviewing hard data and last quarter's results.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you handle 'office politics'?

options.A: I ignore it and focus on the mission.
options.B: I help people coach their way through the tension.
options.C: I bring everyone together to discuss it openly.
options.D: I shut it down if it interferes with output.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the most important part of a meeting?

options.B: The chance for individual growth.
options.C: The moment of consensus.
options.D: The action items and deadlines.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you need to hire someone, you look for:

options.B: Willingness to learn and be coached.
options.C: Ability to collaborate and fit the culture.
options.D: A proven track record of high-speed results.

reflectYourStyle.question: If you have to let someone go, you focus on:

options.A: Ensuring it's the right move for the brand's future.
options.B: Treating them with dignity and helping their transition.
options.C: Discussing the impact on the team's morale.
options.D: Following the legal and performance protocol perfectly.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you handle information sharing?

options.A: I share what is necessary to maintain the vision.
options.B: I share what will help people grow.
options.C: I share everything to maintain transparency.
options.D: I share what is needed to get the job done.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your view on 'Risk' is:

options.B: A great way for people to learn from mistakes.
options.C: Something to be discussed as a group.
options.D: Something to be calculated and minimized.

reflectYourStyle.question: When someone does a great job, you:

options.A: Publicly highlight how they embody the mission.
options.B: Private 1-on-1 praise focusing on their growth.
options.C: Ask the team to celebrate them together.
options.D: Give them a reward and a bigger challenge.

reflectYourStyle.question: You believe leadership is mostly about:

options.B: Development.
options.C: Consensus.
options.D: Execution.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you respond to a missed deadline?

options.A: Ask if the goal is still worth pursuing.
options.B: Ask what skills the person needs to improve.
options.C: Ask the team how they can help catch up.
options.D: Express disappointment and demand a fix.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a new technology arrives, you:

options.A: Imagine how it changes the whole industry.
options.B: Ask who wants to learn it first.
options.C: Discuss as a group if it fits the workflow.
options.D: Analyze if it increases speed and ROI.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a crisis, your communication style is:

options.B: Calm and supportive.
options.C: Open and listening.
options.D: Direct and command-based.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a team is 'drifting', you:

options.B: Coach the individuals who are lagging.
options.C: Hold a 'Reset' meeting for everyone.
options.D: Increase the frequency of reporting.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you view your role in the organization?

options.B: The Gardener.
options.C: The Facilitator.
options.D: The Engine.

reflectYourStyle.question: You think personal growth is:

options.A: Driven by a clear future goal.
options.B: The core of the professional journey.
options.C: Enhanced by team collaboration.
options.D: Measured by performance outcomes.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you have a 'gut feeling', you:

options.A: Follow it and bring the team along.
options.B: Test it by coaching others to see it.
options.C: Propose it to the team for debate.
options.D: Look for data to prove or disprove it.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the biggest waste of time?

options.B: Stunted potential.
options.C: Hidden agendas.
options.D: Inefficiency.

reflectYourStyle.question: When someone is 'stuck', you ask:

options.A: Where do you want to go?
options.B: What are you afraid of?
options.C: What does the team need?
options.D: What is the next step?

reflectYourStyle.question: Your ideal team culture is:

options.B: Safe and developmental.
options.C: Transparent and fair.
options.D: Fast and flawless.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you manage your time?

options.A: I focus on the high-impact future stuff.
options.B: I focus on people and coaching.
options.C: I focus on coordination and alignment.
options.D: I focus on the schedule and tasks.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you disagree with your boss, you:

options.B: Try to coach them to see your view.
options.C: Discuss it with peers first.
options.D: Present data to show why they are wrong.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is your best leadership habit?

options.B: Listening.
options.C: Inclusive planning.
options.D: Setting high bars.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a project ends, you first think of:

options.B: Who learned what.
options.C: How the team feels.
options.D: The final numbers.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you define 'Leadership Presence'?

options.A: Being the most inspiring person in the room.
options.B: Being the most observant person in the room.
options.C: Being the most approachable person in the room.
options.D: Being the most competent person in the room.

reflectYourStyle.question: If you could change one thing in business, it would be:

options.B: The waste of talent.
options.C: The siloed communication.
options.D: The slow pace of change.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you start your day?

options.B: Thinking about the people I'll meet.
options.C: Checking in with the team.
options.D: Reviewing my to-do list.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a high-pressure situation, you:

options.B: Focus on keeping the team calm.
options.C: Focus on group consensus.
options.D: Focus on the execution steps.

reflectYourStyle.question: When delegating power, you:

options.B: Guide them as they handle it.
options.C: Distribute it among the group.
options.D: Grant it based on performance.

reflectYourStyle.question: You handle failure by:

options.B: Reflecting on the growth.
options.C: Discussing it with the team.
options.D: Doing a post-mortem on the data.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the best way to earn respect?

options.B: Being a mentor.
options.C: Being a collaborator.
options.D: Being a top performer.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you're overwhelmed, you:

options.A: Step back and look at the big picture.
options.B: Ask for coaching from a peer.
options.C: Delegate and align with the team.
options.D: Work harder and faster.

reflectYourStyle.question: What motivates you most?

options.B: Impact.
options.C: Involvement.
options.D: Achievement.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your leadership 'legacy' will be:

options.B: The people I grew.
options.C: The culture I built.
options.D: The results I delivered.

reflectYourStyle.question: Finally, you view leadership as a:

options.B: Service.
options.C: Duty.
options.D: Skill.
